resources.string: Function: assigns each x in domain to one y. x–independent, y–dependent. Forms: analytic, tabular, graphical. Notation: y=f(x).
Analytic: formula; Tabular: values; Graphical: plot points. Domain → x, Range → y. Used for calc, observation, visualization.
Even: f(-x)=f(x). Odd: f(-x)=-f(x). Monotone: increasing or decreasing. Constant sign: f(x)>0 or <0. Roots: f(x)=0.
Even → y-axis symmetry; Odd → origin symmetry. Increasing rises, decreasing falls. Roots at x-axis intersections.
Inverse: exists if each y has unique x. f(x)↔g(x). Monotone functions have inverses. Find inverse by solving for x in terms of y.
Graphs symmetric about y=x. M(x₀,y₀) ↔ N(y₀,x₀). Increasing↔increasing, decreasing↔decreasing. Used for checking inverses.
Basic: Linear y=kx+b, Inverse y=k/x, Quadratic y=ax²+bx+c, Power y=xⁿ, Logarithmic y=logₐx. Shapes: line, hyperbola, parabola.
|f(x)| reflects negative part over x-axis. Linear/quadratic shape from coefficients. Hyperbola position → sign of k. Parabola opens up (a>0) or down (a<0).
Solve f(x)=0 → find x-intercepts. g(x)=h(x) → intersection points. Circle: x²+y²=r². Systems: intersections of graphs; lines → one, none, or ∞ solutions.
Solve f(x)>0 → x where graph above axis. Systems: intersection of solution sets. In 2 vars → half-planes; system → overlap region.
